verification_artifacts: mfa_policy: explicit MFA exceptions for legacy ERP and warehouse terminal
ir_plan: no evidence of quarterly testing
encryption_audit: covers encryption and TLS but not availability, processing integrity, privacy, or vendor/change management
results.finding: The evidence shows confirmed gaps (MFA exceptions, untested IR plan) and covers only a subset of SOC2 Trust Service Criteria, making a claim of meeting all evaluated areas unsupported.
results.promotion_rule: An independent SOC2 Type II audit by a licensed CPA covering all five Trust Service Criteria with no qualified findings would confirm this claim.

verification_artifacts: mfa_policy: explicitly lists exceptions for SAP R/3 legacy ERP system and warehouse management terminal, both password-only until at least Q3 2026
results.finding: The MFA policy document directly contradicts the 'ALL systems' claim by documenting active exceptions for at least two named systems.
results.promotion_rule: Removal of all MFA exceptions and technical evidence confirming MFA is enforced across every system, including former exceptions, would be required to upgrade this claim.

verification_artifacts: encryption_audit: 'All data stores: AES-256 encryption confirmed' with no findings noted
results.finding: The January 2026 encryption audit explicitly confirms AES-256 encryption on all data stores with zero findings.

verification_artifacts: encryption_audit: confirms TLS 1.3 on API endpoints and deprecation of TLS 1.2 as of December 2025, but does not address internal service-to-service traffic or third-party integrations
results.finding: The audit confirms TLS 1.3 for API endpoints but does not provide evidence covering internal service-to-service traffic or third-party integrations, leaving the 'ALL data in transit' claim partially supported.
results.promotion_rule: Network scanning results and traffic analysis confirming TLS 1.3 across all internal and external connections, including service-to-service and third-party integrations, would confirm this claim.

verification_artifacts: ir_plan: Incident Response Plan v3.2 exists, last revised September 2024, has a named plan owner (VP Engineering)
results.finding: A documented incident response plan exists at version 3.2 with a revision date and named owner, satisfying the existence requirement.

verification_artifacts: ir_plan: only one tabletop exercise recorded (January 2025); next exercise is listed as TBD and explicitly postponed due to Q1 hiring freeze
results.finding: The IR plan document shows only one exercise in the available record period and confirms the next exercise has been postponed indefinitely, directly contradicting a claim of quarterly testing.
results.promotion_rule: Test records documenting at least four completed exercises within the audit period, including dates, participants, and scenarios, would be required to confirm quarterly testing.

verification_artifacts: mfa_policy: MFA exceptions on two named systems
ir_plan: IR testing postponed, only one exercise evidenced
encryption_audit: scope limited to encryption/TLS; no evidence covering availability, processing integrity, privacy, vendor management, or change management
results.finding: Multiple confirmed gaps — active MFA exceptions and lapsed IR testing — combined with the absence of evidence across several SOC2 Trust Service Criteria indicate Company X is not ready for a SOC2 audit.
results.promotion_rule: A gap assessment by a qualified SOC2 readiness firm covering all five Trust Service Criteria with no material findings, plus remediation of identified MFA and IR testing gaps, would be required to support this claim.
